metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose 'asserting the uploader is preserved' with a concrete structured assertion, e.g.: `curl -u $BOT $GERRIT/changes/$CHANGE_ID/detail | jq -e '.owner.account_id == $UPLOADER_ID'` where `$UPLOADER_ID` is captured before the rebase call.
findings.finding: AC5's smoke test says 'asserting the uploader is preserved' but provides no concrete structured command — it is a prose assertion with no jq/curl command against the Gerrit REST API, making the assertion unverifiable in-session (taxonomy item 6: prose-vs-structured assertion target).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('Tests')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the AC3 autolander smoke test script to the Files section (e.g., `infra/gerrit/autolander-smoke-check.sh` — CREATE) so the integration checkpoint (create scratch change → rebase-on-behalf → assert uploader → submit → abandon) is a named, reviewable deliverable.
findings.finding: AC3 describes a new multi-step integration smoke test (create synthetic change on scratch ref, call rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader REST API, assert uploader is preserved, submit, then abandon/clean the scratch change) but no file implementing this test is enumerated in the Files section. The existing `infra/gerrit/smoke-check.sh` covers only the review-bot; the autolander smoke steps are distinct and require a new or extended script.
findings.location: Files section / AC3 (Tests)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Files: `infra/gerrit/autolander-smoke-check.sh` — CREATE: bash script that (1) reads AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN from SSM, (2) pushes a throwaway commit to refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander as a test uploader, (3) calls POST /changes/{id}/rebase with on_behalf_of_uploader=true as RebarBotNava, (4) asserts the uploader account is unchanged, (5) calls POST /changes/{id}/submit, (6) abandons or deletes the scratch ref. Exit non-zero on any step failure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC5 (smoke test) to specify: (1) create `scratch-autolander` branch before pushing; (2) resolve the submit-then-abandon contradiction — either abandon the change *before* submit (verifying rebase-on-behalf only) and clean up with `POST /abandon`, or if submit is required, clean up by deleting/resetting the scratch branch post-merge (since a MERGED change cannot be abandoned).
findings.finding: The AC5 smoke test says the bot 'submits' the throwaway change and then 'abandons/cleans up the scratch change' — but a MERGED (submitted) change cannot be abandoned in Gerrit. This is a self-contradicting cleanup sequence. Additionally, the smoke test targets 'refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander' but does not specify creating the `scratch-autolander` branch first; without it, the push will be rejected by Gerrit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests (AC3), last bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC5 test flow to: (1) `gerrit create-branch rebar scratch-autolander main` (idempotent); (2) push the synthetic commit to `refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander`; (3) bot rebases-on-behalf and asserts uploader is preserved; (4) **abandon** (do NOT submit) the change via `POST /a/changes/<n>/abandon`; (5) optionally delete the scratch branch. This keeps cleanup simple and avoids the merged-change abandonment impossibility. If submit must be proven, replace step 4 with submit + `DELETE /projects/rebar/branches/scratch-autolander` to clean the branch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing the `rebase = group Contributors` project.config stanza, capture a concrete artifact confirming the `rebase` per-ref ACL token exists and maps to REST `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` in Gerrit 3.14.1 (e.g., a live `GET /a/access/?project=rebar` response showing the right, or a quote from the 3.14.x access-control docs URL) — and reconcile/update the parent ticket's divergent file_impact description.
findings.finding: The plan's central deliverable — `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors` in project.config enabling REST rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader — is an unverified capability assertion. The plan cites 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' but no such confirmation artifact, URL, or captured doc exists in the repo. No `rebase =` line appears anywhere in infra/gerrit/project.config or setup-project.sh. Moreover, the parent ticket's file_impact still references the OLD mechanism ('All-Projects [capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader + submit on refs/heads/*'), indicating a recent design pivot from the global-capability to per-ref-ACL approach whose correctness on Gerrit 3.14.1 is unconfirmed. Gerrit's access-control model is version-specific; the exact config-file token name and its binding to the REST API's `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` field should be verified against the live instance's documentation before the plan is committed.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete)' bullet; Files: infra/gerrit/project.config, infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Run `curl -u admin:<pw> https://<host>/a/access/?project=All-Projects` and `?project=rebar` on the live instance, confirm `rebase` appears as a permission type in the response (alongside its label and REST binding), capture the output as a comment in setup-project.sh, then update the parent ticket file_impact to reflect the per-ref ACL approach.

findings.finding: The plan asserts the Gerrit 'Rebase' access right on this instance (Gerrit 3.14.1) enables rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader via the REST API — citing 'confirmed in this instance's access-control.html' — but then immediately defers the exact access-right name and project.config stanza to implementation-time verification. No captured doc artifact, URL, or concrete project.config snippet is present in the plan or repo. This is an unresolved capability assertion: the central deliverable cannot be authored without first resolving what the plan explicitly leaves open.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet; Files: infra/gerrit/project.config + infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh
findings.suggested_fix:
Consult `https://<gerrit-host>/Documentation/access-control.html` on the live 3.14.1 instance, confirm the exact permission token name (likely `rebase` in lowercase, not `label-Rebase`), then add the verified snippet to the plan body under the Rebase AC:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  rebase = group Contributors   # or a dedicated bot group
```
and note: 'Verified against Gerrit 3.14.1 access-control.html on <date>'. This eliminates the implementation-time ambiguity and ensures the project.config push succeeds with the right semantics.

findings.finding: The `Rebase` access-right config-file token for project.config is asserted to exist on this Gerrit 3.14.x instance but is unverified — no `rebase =` line appears anywhere in infra/gerrit/project.config or setup-project.sh, and the plan itself defers confirmation to implementation time ('Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form … against this instance's access-control.html'). If the ACL token differs from what the plan expects (e.g., it requires a capability-level grant or a plugin-specific name), the project.config push will either be silently ignored or rejected, leaving the bot unable to rebase on behalf of the uploader.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' + Files: infra/gerrit/project.config
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit pre-implementation step: `curl -u admin:<token> https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/a/access/rebar | python3 -m json.tool | grep -i rebase` — if the key appears in the response, record it; if absent, probe All-Projects. Encode the confirmed token in project.config and setup-project.sh before merging the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC1, e.g.: `aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token --query 'Parameter.Type' --output text | grep -x SecureString` to produce evidence the parameter exists with the right type before proceeding to AC5.
findings.finding: AC1 describes adding the SSM parameter and fetching it via `fetch-secrets.sh`, but provides no concrete proving command to verify the parameter exists at the correct path and is a SecureString — the AC can be 'done' with no evidence.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('Credential')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a branch-creation step to the AC5 smoke test spec (e.g., `PUT /a/projects/rebar/branches/scratch-autolander`) before pushing to `refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander`, and a corresponding delete step in cleanup.
findings.finding: The AC5 smoke test pushes to `refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander` but does not specify creating the target branch `refs/heads/scratch-autolander` first — Gerrit rejects pushes to `refs/for/refs/heads/<branch>` when the target branch does not exist on the server.
findings.location: AC (Tests / AC3 smoke test): 'creates a synthetic throwaway change on a scratch ref (e.g. refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander)'
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend to the smoke test: 'Create the scratch branch via `curl -X PUT -u $BOT_USER:$TOKEN https://$GERRIT_HOST/a/projects/rebar/branches/scratch-autolander -d \'{}\''`, and append to cleanup: 'Delete it via `DELETE /a/projects/rebar/branches/scratch-autolander`'.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan (Files section or a note) that `user_data.sh` is an intentional non-consumer of `autolander-gerrit-token` (because the autolander reads the compose `.env`, not `/etc/rebar/.env`) to resolve the ambiguity raised by ssm.tf's 'keep in sync with user_data.sh' comment.
findings.finding: The ssm.tf comment (line 16) explicitly says 'keep in sync with user_data.sh', and user_data.sh has its own separate `declare -A PARAMS` that includes `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (a parallel mapping to fetch-secrets.sh's coverage of the same param). The plan adds `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` only to fetch-secrets.sh but never mentions user_data.sh as a non-consumer — leaving it ambiguous whether user_data.sh is an unenumerated consumer or intentionally excluded.
findings.location: Files section / infra/terraform/user_data.sh

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the smoke test's synthetic commit must include a `Signed-off-by:` trailer (matching the `requireSignedOffBy = true` enforcement in project.config) to pass the DCO gate before the change can be created.
findings.finding: The AC5 smoke test pushes a synthetic throwaway change to `refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander`, but the live project.config enforces `requireSignedOffBy = true` under `[receive]` — Gerrit will reject the push with a DCO error before the change is created, silently breaking the smoke test unless the test commit includes a `Signed-off-by:` trailer.
findings.location: AC (Tests / AC3 smoke test): 'creates a synthetic throwaway change on a scratch ref (e.g. refs/for/refs/heads/scratch-autolander)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC5 smoke-test spec: 'The throwaway commit MUST include a `Signed-off-by: <autolander-bot-email>` trailer; use `git commit --trailer "Signed-off-by: RebarBotNava <autolander@rebar.solutions.navateam.com>"` when creating the synthetic change, matching the DCO requirement in project.config [receive] requireSignedOffBy = true.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the per-service env-var scoping posture for the shared `.env`: either (a) explicitly accept the full-pool exposure with a documented rationale (e.g., 'all services run as the same trust principal on a single-tenant host'), OR (b) scope `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` to the autolander service only (via `environment:` key rather than `env_file:`, or a service-specific env file) so the review-bot container does not receive the autolander's submit-capable credential.
findings.finding: The plan adds AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN (a credential with Submit + Rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader rights on refs/heads/*) to the shared `.env` consumed wholesale by all compose services via `env_file: .env`, but never declares per-service env-var scoping. The review-bot container already reads the full `.env` (docker-compose.yml line 81); after this plan it will also have access to the autolander's privileged Gerrit token. The autolander will similarly receive GERRIT_ADMIN_PASSWORD, GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc. — widening the blast radius of any single-container compromise.
findings.location: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh (shared .env output) / AC: Credential bullet ('fetched by fetch-secrets.sh into the shared .env as AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN') / infra/compose/docker-compose.yml line 81 (env_file: .env)
findings.suggested_fix: In the docker-compose.yml autolander service definition, inject AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN via the `environment:` stanza (reading from a shell variable or a service-specific .env fragment) rather than including it in the shared `env_file: .env`. Alternatively, add a note in the plan explicitly accepting the shared-pool exposure as a deliberate single-trust-principal design decision, so the access posture is declared rather than silent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a remediation branch to AC2: state the exact command to add `RebarBotNava` to `Contributors` if the membership check fails (e.g., the Gerrit REST call or the `setup-project.sh` variable to set), so the executor never needs to stop and ask.
findings.finding: AC2 uses 'verify RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors' as a check with no stated remediation branch — if the verification fails (the bot is NOT in Contributors), the executor has no instruction and must stop to ask; this is a placeholder deferring a real design decision.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('Submit is already covered')
findings.suggested_fix: Add after the verify line: 'If not present, add via: `ssh -p 29418 gerrit gerrit set-members Contributors --add RebarBotNava` (or set `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS=RebarBotNava` in `setup-project.sh` and re-run). The plan MUST NOT proceed to AC3 until this passes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan title to remove 'All-Projects capability' (or replace with 'rebar-project Rebase ACL') so the title matches the body's explicit NOT-All-Projects instruction.
findings.finding: The plan's title says 'All-Projects capability' but the body explicitly states 'NOT All-Projects' — a conflicting signal that could cause an executor to mis-target the ACL edit.
findings.location: Title vs. Files section and AC3 body
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebar-Project Rebase ACL + submit ACL provisioning'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan to reflect what is actually being delivered (e.g. 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase ACL provisioning') so the title does not contradict AC2's 'no submit grant' and AC3's 'NOT All-Projects / NOT a global capability' constraints.
findings.finding: The plan title ('S5b Credentials (SSM) + All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning') names two deliverables — an All-Projects capability grant and a submit ACL — that the plan body explicitly rejects: AC3 says 'NOT `All-Projects`', 'NOT a global `[capability]`', and AC2 says 'do NOT add a submit grant.' This creates a direct contradiction between the title framing and the body's constraints, and implies orphan 'deliverables' in the title with no covering work.
findings.location: Plan title vs. AC2 and AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Change the title to: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + Rebase-on-behalf ACL provisioning' — dropping 'All-Projects capability' and 'submit ACL provisioning', which the body explicitly rejects.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE section (or teardown AC) documenting how to remove the `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` SSM parameter from the `for_each` set and delete the `rebase = group Contributors` Gerrit ACL stanza when the autolander bot is decommissioned.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for the `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` SSM parameter or the `rebase = group Contributors` Gerrit ACL stanza. If the autolander bot is decommissioned, both pieces of shared state persist indefinitely with no documented removal procedure.
findings.location: Files: `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, `infra/gerrit/project.config` / `setup-project.sh`; AC (Credential) and AC (Rebase-on-behalf)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Teardown' AC: 'To decommission: remove `autolander-gerrit-token` from `local.rebar_secret_params` in ssm.tf (Terraform will then delete the parameter on next apply), and remove the `rebase = group Contributors` stanza from project.config / re-run setup-project.sh.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan to reflect what it actually delivers (e.g. 'S5b: Gerrit credential (SSM) + rebar-project Rebase ACL for auto-lander bot') so the title does not promise All-Projects or submit provisioning that the body explicitly forbids.
findings.finding: The plan title promises 'All-Projects capability + submit ACL provisioning' but the body explicitly does neither: it avoids All-Projects entirely (Rebase grant goes to the rebar project's own refs/meta/config) and explicitly forbids adding a submit grant ('do NOT add a submit grant'). An implementer reading the title first may expect — or attempt — both of those actions, contradicting the body's explicit prohibitions.
findings.location: Plan title and AC2/AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Change the title to something like: 'S5b Credentials (SSM) + rebar-project Rebase ACL provisioning' — dropping 'All-Projects capability' and 'submit ACL provisioning' since both are explicitly out of scope per the body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and explicitly name the Gerrit 3.14.1 REST endpoint and request body for rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader (expected: `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}`) against this instance's `/Documentation/rest-api-changes.html#rebase-change` before committing the smoke test spec.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 smoke test asserts the bot calls a rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader REST endpoint, but `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` with `on_behalf_of_uploader: true` in the request body is a new, unused integration surface in this codebase — no existing script exercises this endpoint — and it is not verified against this instance's Gerrit 3.14.1 API surface.
findings.location: AC (Rebase-on-behalf access right / AC3 smoke test): 'confirms the token materializes into .env, that RebarBotNava authenticates + rebases it on-behalf (asserting the uploader is preserved)'

findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers the exact `project.config` access-right token for on-behalf-of-uploader rebase to 'verify at implementation against this instance's access-control.html' but specifies no fallback protocol if the `Rebase` right is absent, renamed, or works differently in this Gerrit version. This is an unspecified fallback for a known-uncertain sub-step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)': 'Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form … then encode it in project.config'

findings.finding: The plan specifies that `fetch-secrets.sh` should fetch `autolander-gerrit-token` into `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` but does not specify whether to use `get_param` (required/fail-fast) or `get_param_optional` (soft/non-blocking). The existing script makes this distinction explicit for every token; the wrong choice has direct reliability consequences (a missing token silently produces a blank env-var vs. aborting boot).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Credential' bullet: 'fetched by fetch-secrets.sh into the shared .env as AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN' / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Use `get_param_optional` with a downstream fail-loud guard in the autolander container's entrypoint (similar to the OAUTH pattern), so the review-bot container can still start if the autolander token is not yet provisioned — decoupling deployment ordering.

findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `autolander-gerrit-token` should be fetched as a required (`get_param`, fail-fast) or optional (`get_param_optional`) secret in `fetch-secrets.sh`. Using `get_param` (required) couples every boot of the single shared instance to the token being pre-populated — even before the autolander container is deployed — which could block instance bring-up. Using `get_param_optional` silently starts the autolander container with a blank token. The plan's AC says 'fetched by `fetch-secrets.sh` into the shared `.env` as `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`' but does not resolve required-vs-optional.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Credential bullet; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Use `get_param_optional` in `fetch-secrets.sh` (matching `reviewbot-tickets-pat`), and add an explicit CHANGEME/blank guard inside the autolander container startup (e.g., entrypoint exits non-zero if `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` is empty), so the failure is loud at the service level rather than the boot level.

findings.finding: The plan does not declare whether AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN should be fetched as *required* (fail-fast, like gerrit-bot-token) or *optional* (silent blank, like reviewbot-tickets-pat) in fetch-secrets.sh. If treated as optional, the autolander container will start with an empty credential and every Gerrit API call will 401 silently — an undeclared access posture on the container→Gerrit boundary.
findings.location: AC — Credential bullet / infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh edit
findings.suggested_fix: In the Files / fetch-secrets.sh section, add: 'Use get_param (required, fail-fast), matching the treatment of gerrit-bot-token — the autolander cannot function without this credential so a CHANGEME or absent value must abort boot.'

findings.finding: The Rebase AC is partially effort-framed: it instructs the executor to 'verify... then encode it in project.config' rather than stating the specific observable outcome (what the project.config entry looks like when correct). This makes the criterion's pass/fail state dependent on executor judgment rather than a checkable artifact.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet

findings.finding: No concrete proving commands are given for any AC: credential materialization, group-membership verification, rebase-on-behalf API call, idempotency re-run, or submit. The smoke-check AC describes outcomes in prose ('confirms ... uploader preserved') without a single executable command, leaving done-ness unverifiable without author judgment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all four bullets, especially 'Tests' bullet

findings.finding: None of the three acceptance criteria include a concrete proving command. AC1 (SSM/env) has no aws-cli or grep command; AC2 (capability grant) has no Gerrit SSH/REST query; AC3 (smoke) uses 'confirms' without specifying the command, target URL, or expected HTTP status — leaving done-ness unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet; all three AC checkboxes

findings.finding: The Rebase AC explicitly defers the exact access-right name and config form to implementation-time investigation ('Verify at implementation the exact access-right name/form... against this instance's access-control.html... then encode it in project.config'). An executing agent cannot write the project.config entry or setup-project.sh grant without first stopping to resolve this Gerrit-version-specific question — a real placeholder deferring a design decision to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' bullet
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a verified project.config snippet to the plan body, e.g.:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  rebase = group RebarBotNava
```
and note the Gerrit version it was confirmed against (check `access-control.html` once, record the result here rather than deferring to the executor).

findings.finding: AC3's 'bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance' does not specify what test change is used (pre-existing change ID, synthetically created change, specific repo), making the smoke-test non-deterministic and potentially environment-destructive if the wrong change is submitted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'the bot can authenticate + submit a test change on the instance'

findings.finding: The plan mandates a `Rebase` access right in `project.config` on `refs/heads/*` but never specifies which GROUP is granted the right. The `setup-project.sh` `want=` dict (line 123) maps exactly four groups; no new group for Rebase holders is named, nor is an existing group (e.g. `Service Users` or `Contributors`) designated. Without the group name the `project.config` stanza and `groups` file cannot be written, and the `want=` dict cannot be updated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism)' / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123 / infra/gerrit/project.config [access "refs/heads/*"]
findings.suggested_fix: Either reuse `Service Users` (since `RebarBotNava` is provisioned there via `service-user.sh`) and add `rebase = group Service Users` to `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, or create a new `Autolanders` group, name it in the plan, add it to `setup-project.sh`'s group-creation block and `want=` dict, and add `rebase = group Autolanders` to `project.config`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
